metadata.title: Recruitment Industry Knowledge Base
metadata.description: Comprehensive recruitment industry data compiled from major job boards, recruitment marketing platforms, TA tech products, and industry reports from 2025-2026
metadata.update_notes: Major update with latest data from Appcast 2026, SHRM 2025, iCIMS 2026, BLS JOLTS Dec 2025, Indeed Hiring Lab Feb 2026, WordStream/LOCALiQ 2025, IMF Jan 2026, Gartner 2026, Deloitte 2026 Global Human Capital Trends, PwC AI Jobs Barometer 2025, McKinsey AI workforce research, and SIA staffing forecasts

cost_per_click.description: Average cost-per-click (CPC) benchmarks for recruitment advertising across platforms and channels

indeed.free_posts: Up to 3 free job postings per month (30 days each, lower visibility)
indeed.notes: CPC varies heavily by industry, role seniority, and geography. Best for volume hiring, blue-collar, and hourly roles.

linkedin.notes: Premium CPC reflects highly targeted professional audience. Best for white-collar, professional, and executive roles.

ziprecruiter.notes: Uses AI-powered matching. Custom pricing for enterprise. Q4 2025 revenue: $111.7M.

google_search_ads.cpc_trend: CPC has risen for 87% of industries; sharper increases on smart bidding campaigns
google_search_ads.notes: WordStream/LOCALiQ 2025 benchmark from 16,446 US campaigns (Apr 2024-Mar 2025). 65% of industries saw better conversion rates despite rising CPCs.

meta_facebook_ads.notes: Recruitment/employment has very low CPC but also lowest CTR (0.47%) among all industries. However, CVR at 11.73% is among the highest, suggesting high intent among those who do click. Facebook Lead Ads format is especially effective for recruitment.
meta_facebook_ads._source: WordStream/LOCALiQ Facebook Ads Benchmarks 2025, Triple Whale

by_industry_appcast.description: Recruitment-specific CPC data from Appcast Benchmark Reports (2025 analyzing 2024 data, 2026 analyzing 2025 data)
by_industry_appcast.overall_cpc_trend_2025: Continued upward pressure driven by job board pricing shifts and programmatic auction dynamics
by_industry_appcast.notes: CPCs rose over 27% in 2024 despite expectations. In 2025, costs continued rising even as labor market softened, attributed to job board pricing model changes and shrinking organic reach pushing employers into pay-to-play.
by_industry_appcast._source: Appcast 2025 and 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Reports

cost_per_application.description: Average cost-per-application (CPA) benchmarks for recruitment advertising

appcast_2025_report.vs_2023: Lower CPA in every single state compared to 2023
appcast_2025_report.sitting_vs_standing: Sitting-down jobs (tech, marketing) have higher apply rates and CPAs than standing-up jobs (construction, retail)

appcast_2026_report.overall_cpa_trend_2025: Rose sharply despite softer labor market
appcast_2026_report.apply_rates: Remained high due to increased job-seeker volume and smarter recruitment marketing (better targeting, placements, apply experiences)
appcast_2026_report.white_collar_apply_rates: Rising significantly (white-collar recession deepening); tech apply rate surged to 7.14%
appcast_2026_report.remote_hybrid_advantage: Narrowing as remote work becomes normalized; no longer a premium hook for candidate attraction
appcast_2026_report.key_insight: Volume no longer guarantees efficiency. Higher apply rates led to more screening which paradoxically increased cost-per-hire, especially in tech.
appcast_2026_report.disposition_data: First-ever candidate disposition benchmarks show channels with inflated CPAs and low interview yield vs channels that consistently produce hireable talent
appcast_2026_report.quality_vs_quantity: Job boards may drive top-of-funnel volume. Search and social may drive higher-quality candidates. Disposition data shows the right blend.
appcast_2026_report._source: Appcast 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report (10th Annual)

google_ads_benchmark.cpl_trend_2025: 13 of 23 industries saw CPL increases, but average increase was only ~5% (down from 25% prior year)
google_ads_benchmark.notes: Career & Employment saw one of the steepest CPL drops (-46.74% YoY) but also the biggest CVR decline (-23.09% YoY), suggesting high volatility in this vertical.

joveo_historical.notes: Joveo advocates shifting to CPQA (Cost Per Qualified Applicant) as a better metric than CPC/CPA

apply_rates.description: Apply rate benchmarks (percentage of clicks that convert to applications)

appcast_2025.trend: Remained high, fueled by increased job-seeker volume and smarter recruitment marketing
appcast_2025.white_collar_roles: Rising significantly as white-collar recession deepened
appcast_2025.frontline_roles: Remained costly and hard to fill (especially healthcare)
appcast_2025.remote_hybrid_advantage: Narrowing as remote work becomes normalized; 'remote' no longer a premium hook
appcast_2025.notes: Higher apply rates in tech did not improve quality; recruiter workloads ballooned and CPH increased as more screening was needed.

career_site_benchmark.non_conversion_rate: 95% of career site visits do not result in a completed application
career_site_benchmark.reasons: Brand awareness, competitive landscape, targeting and timing, career site experience

shrm_2025.recruiter_workload: Over half of organizations have recruiters managing ~20 requisitions each; higher at larger firms
shrm_2025.qoh_tracking: Only 20% of organizations effectively track quality of hire
shrm_2025.ai_in_recruiting: 64% of orgs using AI for HR use it for recruiting, interviewing, and/or hiring
shrm_2025.ai_candidate_usage: 40-80% of job applicants use AI to write resumes, craft cover letters, and prepare for interviews
shrm_2025.ai_recruiter_expectations: 78% predict candidates will increasingly use AI to apply; 73% expect rise in AI for recruiting tasks

shrm_2026.notes: Average cost per hire has increased over past three years, correlating with increased use of generative AI. Varies widely by company size, role type, and industry.

appcast_2024.trend: Softer and flat throughout 2024 with seasonal push at year-end

appcast_2025.cost_per_hire_trend: Rose sharply despite softer labor market
appcast_2025.notes: In tech and other white-collar fields, higher apply rates led to more screening, paradoxically increasing CPH. CPH increased again YoY in tech.

cost_per_hire.hidden_costs: 38% of SMBs underestimate their hiring costs
cost_per_hire.ai_impact: Companies report 30% reduction in cost-per-hire with AI tools
cost_per_hire.programmatic_impact: Companies using programmatic platforms report ~50% reduction in cost per applicant and 33% better applicant quality

icims_2025.notes: Despite more candidate options, employers aren't moving faster. Decision-making speed not keeping pace with market activity. If apply-to-screen rates, interview capacity, and time-to-first-response don't improve, requisition aging will rise.
icims_2025.hiring_funnel_issue: Apply-to-hire ratios are rising but hiring flow is broken; strong talent interest but measured mobility
icims_2025.hospitality: Highest application abandonment and interview no-show rates (leaky hiring funnel)
icims_2025.healthcare: Demand hot spot with a conversion problem; openings and applications recovering but hires still lag

goodtime_2025.increase_in_time_to_hire: 60% of companies reported an increase (up from 44% in 2023)
goodtime_2025.reduced_time_to_hire: Only 6% of employers were able to reduce

time_to_fill.ai_impact: 25% faster time-to-hire reported with AI tools

job_boards.employer_usage: 68.6% conduct all/most hiring through job boards

employee_referrals.quality_multiplier: 4x more high-quality candidates vs job boards
employee_referrals.likelihood_of_hire: 18x more likely to get the job

sourced_outbound.hire_likelihood: 5x more likely to be hired than inbound applicants
sourced_outbound.passive_candidate_pool: 70% of global candidate pool are passive

icims_2025.ratio: 33 applicants per opening (up from 28 prior year)
icims_2025.notes: APO at series high gives employers more qualified candidates per requisition but increases risk of bottlenecks if teams don't respond quickly enough. Confirms near-term buyer's market dynamic in many roles.

greenhouse_2025.trend: Nearly 3x more applications per role vs 2021

google_search_ads_2025.ctr_trend: Increasing YoY for five years; driven by larger ads, more ads above fold, ads blending with organic
google_search_ads_2025.notes: 65% of industries saw better conversion rates in 2025. Career & Employment is among the most volatile verticals.

meta_facebook_ads_2025.notes: Employment/recruitment has very low CTR but one of the highest CVRs on Facebook, suggesting high intent among clickers. Lead Ads format particularly effective.

recruitment_funnel_benchmarks.career_site_visit_to_apply: 5% (95% of visits don't result in completed application)
recruitment_funnel_benchmarks.hospitality_funnel_leakage: Highest application abandonment and interview no-show rates (iCIMS)
recruitment_funnel_benchmarks.healthcare_conversion_problem: Openings and applications recovering but hires still lag (iCIMS)

ai_in_recruiting.title: AI in Recruiting
ai_in_recruiting.description: Artificial intelligence adoption and impact in talent acquisition

use_cases.ai_assisted_messaging: +9% more likely to make quality hire (LinkedIn)
use_cases.quality_of_hire_measurement: 61% of TA pros believe AI can improve QoH measurement
use_cases.initial_screening_2026: AI will handle 95% of initial candidate screening in 2026
use_cases.agentic_ai_in_recruiting: 52% of talent leaders plan to add AI agents to their teams in 2026
use_cases.high_volume_hiring: AI-first hiring for high-volume, low-complexity roles (retail, customer service, drivers) has highest cost savings potential (Gartner)
use_cases._source: LinkedIn Future of Recruiting 2025, Gartner TA Trends 2026, Korn Ferry TA Trends 2026

candidate_side.job_seekers_using_gen_ai: 53% of new hires used GenAI in job search (Q1 2024)

gartner_predictions_2026.ai_proficiency_hiring_2027: 75% of hiring processes will include certifications and tests for workplace AI proficiency
gartner_predictions_2026.ai_free_assessments: 50% of global organizations will require AI-free skills assessments due to atrophy of critical thinking from GenAI use (through 2026)
gartner_predictions_2026.hr_operating_model_ai_impact: 29% predicted productivity gains from AI in HR operating model (highest impact area)
gartner_predictions_2026.recruiter_role_redesign: As AI handles low-complexity work, recruiters must advise on talent strategy and role design for hard-to-find skills
gartner_predictions_2026.candidate_fraud: Organizations face overburdened, fraud-ridden processes; combining high-touch approaches with AI tools
gartner_predictions_2026.internal_marketplaces_2026: One-third of recruiting effort will shift to internal talent marketplaces
gartner_predictions_2026.redeployment_by_2030: 1 in 5 employees will need to be redeployed
gartner_predictions_2026.ai_creates_more_jobs_2028: Gartner projects AI will create more jobs than it destroys by 2028
gartner_predictions_2026.genai_trough_of_disillusionment: GenAI in HR quickly moving toward Trough of Disillusionment; many CHROs struggling to demonstrate true value
gartner_predictions_2026.agentic_ai_status: Still in infancy; ML in HR and AI in TA entering Slope of Enlightenment
gartner_predictions_2026._source: Gartner TA Trends 2026, Gartner Future of Work Trends 2026, Gartner Strategic Predictions 2026

deloitte_2026_human_capital.survey_size: 9,000+ business and HR leaders across 89 countries
deloitte_2026_human_capital.key_theme: Shift from tensions to tipping points; intentional choices for continuous adaptation
deloitte_2026_human_capital.ai_gap: Gap between AI potential and reality remains wide; requires evolving work design, worker relevance, and leadership culture
deloitte_2026_human_capital.workers_open_to_ai_offloading: 70% of workers open to offloading work to AI to free up time and boost creativity
deloitte_2026_human_capital.agentic_ai_pilots_2025: 1 in 4 companies using GenAI will launch agentic AI pilots
deloitte_2026_human_capital.ai_in_hiring_usage: Over 60% of US organizations now use AI-based tools for at least one stage of hiring
deloitte_2026_human_capital._source: Deloitte 2026 Global Human Capital Trends Report

pwc_ai_jobs_barometer.ai_wage_premium_2024: 56% (more than double the 25% premium from prior year)
pwc_ai_jobs_barometer.job_growth_in_ai_exposed_roles: 38% job growth in roles with high automation potential (2019-2024)
pwc_ai_jobs_barometer.degree_requirement_decline: Dropped from 66% (2019) to 59% (2024) for AI-augmented jobs
pwc_ai_jobs_barometer.agent_exposed_skill_obsolescence: 66% skill obsolescence forecast for agent-exposed roles

mckinsey_ai_workforce.organizations_using_ai: 88% use AI in at least one business function; only 1% have achieved true AI maturity
mckinsey_ai_workforce.knowledge_workers_using_ai: 75% already use AI tools, often without formal company deployment
mckinsey_ai_workforce.skills_based_hiring_productivity: Employees hired based on skills are 30% more productive in first six months vs degree-based hires
mckinsey_ai_workforce.us_economic_potential: $2.9 trillion/year added to US economy by 2030 if organizations redesign workflows for AI agents and robots
mckinsey_ai_workforce.work_hours_automatable: 57% of US work hours could theoretically be automated by current tech
mckinsey_ai_workforce.skills_relevance: 70%+ of skills sought by employers are used in both automatable and non-automatable work, suggesting most skills remain relevant but how/where they're used will evolve
mckinsey_ai_workforce.occupational_transitions_by_2030: Up to 12 million in both Europe and US

regulatory.eu_ai_act: Effective 2024, phases through 2026-2027 for high-risk obligations
regulatory.nyc_local_law_144: Annual bias audit and candidate notices required for automated hiring tools
regulatory.ai_ethics_in_hiring: No longer optional; legal, business, and reputational priority. Candidates expect fairness and transparency, regulators increasingly demand it.
regulatory._source: EU legislation, NYC local law, Gartner, Deloitte

programmatic_job_advertising.title: Programmatic Job Advertising
programmatic_job_advertising.description: Automated, data-driven job ad placement and optimization

market_data.private_marketplace_share: 91%+ of US spending through PMPs and direct

key_trends_2026.programmatic_video: $110 billion+ projected (75% of new programmatic ad dollars)
key_trends_2026.first_party_data: 40% of US marketers use as primary privacy-centric targeting
key_trends_2026.agentic_ai: Shift from basic automation to context-aware AI decision-making
key_trends_2026.cpqa_shift: Industry shifting from CPC/CPA toward CPQA (Cost Per Qualified Applicant) as preferred metric, driven by flood of AI-generated applications
key_trends_2026.ai_application_flood: Candidates now apply to hundreds of roles at once using AI, creating 10x more applications but still resulting in just one hire
key_trends_2026.waste_reduction: Up to 40% of job advertising spending is wasted; programmatic platforms help identify and eliminate waste
key_trends_2026.programmatic_roi: Companies using programmatic platforms report ~50% reduction in cost per applicant and 33% better applicant quality

appcast.description: Leading recruitment marketing platform, part of The Stepstone Group

pandologic.description: Leading programmatic job advertising platform (now Veritone Hire)

joveo.description: Programmatic job advertising platform advocating CPQA metric
joveo.recognition: Fosway 2025 AI Market Assessment for TA
joveo.metric_shift: Advocating Cost Per Qualified Applicant (CPQA) over CPC/CPA

recruitics.description: Recruitment marketing analytics and programmatic platform

radancy.description: Comprehensive talent acquisition platform (formerly TMP Worldwide)
radancy.products: Programmatic AdTech, Career Sites, Employee Referral, CRM
radancy.trend_2026: Agentic AI and connected data for smarter candidate engagement
radancy.personalization_expectation: 72% of job seekers expect personalized communication

skills_based_hiring.title: Skills-Based Hiring
skills_based_hiring.description: Shift from degree-based to skills-based hiring practices

adoption.actual_impact_of_degree_removal: Only 0.14% of hires affected by degree removal
adoption.biggest_tech_hiring_trend_2026: Skills-based hiring is the #1 technology hiring trend in 2026
adoption._source: TestGorilla State of Skills-Based Hiring 2025, NACE Job Outlook 2026, iMocha Tech Hiring Trends 2026

degree_requirements.trend: Slightly down from pre-pandemic but rising since March 2024
degree_requirements.pwc_ai_augmented_jobs_degree_decline: Dropped from 66% (2019) to 59% (2024) for AI-augmented jobs
degree_requirements._source: Indeed, NACE, PwC AI Jobs Barometer 2025

outcomes.mckinsey_productivity_gain: Employees hired based on skills are 30% more productive in first 6 months vs degree-based hires

remote_hybrid_work.title: Remote & Hybrid Work Trends
remote_hybrid_work.description: The evolution of work arrangements and their impact on recruitment

retention_impact.stanford_nature_study: Zero negative productivity impact, 33% turnover drop

dei_hiring.title: DEI in Hiring
dei_hiring.description: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion trends in recruitment

political_regulatory_shifts.trump_executive_order_2025: DEI programs put into question at federal level
political_regulatory_shifts.public_reporting_decline: Companies sharing less diversity data publicly
political_regulatory_shifts.dei_washing_concern: Minimal increases in diversity hiring following controversies (Stanford GSB study)

future_trends.companies_reframing_dei: Around talent development and employee engagement to reduce legal risk

internal_mobility.title: Internal Mobility
internal_mobility.description: Internal hiring and career movement within organizations

great_stay_low_hire_low_fire.title: The Great Stay / Low-Hire Low-Fire Market
great_stay_low_hire_low_fire.description: The current labor market characterized by reduced mobility and cautious hiring

key_indicators.quits_rate_dec_2025: 2.0% (3.2 million quits, unchanged; below pre-pandemic levels)
key_indicators.job_openings_dec_2025: 6.5 million (continued trending down; rate fell to 3.9%, lowest since 2017 excluding pandemic)
key_indicators.job_openings_to_unemployed_nov_2025: 0.91 (7.146M openings vs 7.831M unemployed; significantly below pre-pandemic, lowest since March 2021)
key_indicators.indeed_jpi_jan_2026: Largely flat since start of year; never rose above early-Dec 2025 level
key_indicators.job_creation_2025_revised: Only 181,000 jobs added in 2025 (down from initially reported 584,000; weakest since 2003 excluding recession years)
key_indicators.feb_2026_jobs_report: Nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000; labor market averaged essentially zero net job creation over past 6 months
key_indicators.indeed_wage_tracker_2025: Posted wage growth fell from 3.4% to 2.1% over course of 2025
key_indicators._source: BLS JOLTS December 2025, Indeed Hiring Lab, Monster

hiring_disconnect.icims_july_2025: Hiring fell 10% YoY even as openings +5% and applications +7%
hiring_disconnect.bls_job_openings_to_unemployed_nov_2025: 0.91 (below 1.0 for first time since pandemic recovery)
hiring_disconnect.glassdoor_offer_rejection: 12% less likely to reject offers in 2025 vs 2023
hiring_disconnect.job_searches_jan_2026: Up to 31% higher than early-Dec 2025 average (Indeed)
hiring_disconnect.icims_early_2026: Applications, openings, and hires all rising MoM but YoY increases smaller than 2025; softer start, not crashing
hiring_disconnect._source: iCIMS, LinkedIn, BLS, Glassdoor, Indeed Hiring Lab

indeed.name: Indeed

key_data.jpi_jan_2026: Flat; never rose above early-Dec 2025 level
key_data.trend: Declining across most sectors; labor market cooled through 2025
key_data.healthcare_share_of_job_growth_2025: Nearly 75% of all net US job growth
key_data.white_collar_postings: Well below pre-pandemic in tech, media, professional services; tech postings 34% below Feb 2020
key_data.ai_postings_surge: Job postings mentioning AI surged 130%+ above pre-pandemic; tech AI postings 45% above Feb 2020
key_data.posted_wage_growth_trend_2025: Fell from 3.4% to 2.1% over course of 2025
key_data.job_searches_jan_2026: Up to 31% higher than early-Dec 2025 average
key_data.feb_2026_jobs_report: Nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000; zero net job creation over past 6 months
key_data.2025_revised_payrolls: Only 181,000 jobs (down from reported 584,000; weakest since 2003 ex-recession)
key_data.ai_workers_usage: Only 43% of US workers regularly use AI at work; ~40% actively disengaged with AI
key_data.international_sponsorship: Nearly 3x more visa/green card sponsorships vs pre-pandemic

indeed.source: Indeed Hiring Lab 2026 Reports, Indeed AI at Work Report 2025

linkedin.name: LinkedIn
linkedin.source: LinkedIn Global Talent Trends, Future of Recruiting 2025

key_data.forever_layoffs_trend: Rolling small layoffs replacing large cuts

ziprecruiter.name: ZipRecruiter
ziprecruiter.source: ZipRecruiter Third Annual Employer Survey, Q4 2025 Earnings

monster.name: Monster

key_data.acquired_by: JobGet Inc. (job board) and Valnet Inc. (media)

monster.source: Monster 2026 WorkWatch Report, Monster 2026 Job Market Outlook

icims.name: iCIMS

key_data.early_2026: All three metrics rising MoM but YoY increases smaller than 2025; softer start, not crashing
key_data.healthcare_conversion_problem: Openings and applications recovering but hires still lag
key_data.hospitality_funnel_leakage: Highest application abandonment and interview no-show rates
key_data.hiring_funnel_broken: Apply-to-hire ratios rising but hiring flow broken; strong talent interest but measured mobility

icims.source: iCIMS Monthly Workforce Reports 2025-2026, iCIMS State of Frontline Hiring 2025

greenhouse.name: Greenhouse
greenhouse.source: Greenhouse 2025 Workforce & Hiring Report, AI in Hiring Report

key_data.focus: AI-powered efficiency gains for faster hiring

phenom.name: Phenom
phenom.type: TA Tech / Intelligent Talent Experience Platform

key_data.employer_brand: Strong employer brand is non-negotiable in 2025-2026

eightfold_ai.name: Eightfold AI

key_data.products: AI Recruiting, Talent Intelligence, Workforce Transformation, AI Interviewer

eightfold_ai.source: Eightfold AI Company Data, Gartner/Eightfold Reports

beamery.name: Beamery

key_data.focus: AI-powered sourcing, hiring, upskilling, internal mobility

beamery.source: Beamery Forrester ROI Study, SelectHub Reviews

key_data.capabilities: AI analysis of responses, facial expressions, speech patterns

appcast.name: Appcast

key_data.new_in_2026: First-ever candidate disposition benchmarks and international hiring benchmarks

appcast.source: Appcast 2025 and 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Reports

key_data.top_growing_sectors: Insurance (+28%), BPO/ITES (+22%), Real Estate (+19%), Hospitality (+15%), Retail (+14%)

stepstone_totaljobs.name: StepStone / Totaljobs

key_data.career_changers_hiring_2026: Two-thirds of companies plan to hire
key_data.platform_migration: Year-long migration and European unification strategy

stepstone_totaljobs.source: Totaljobs Hiring Trends 2026, StepStone Salary Report 2026

unemployment_rate.imf_forecast: Close to 4% in 2026-2027 given slowing population growth
unemployment_rate._source: BLS Employment Situation, Indeed Hiring Lab, JP Morgan, IMF

job_openings.jolts_dec_2025: 6.5 million (trending down; rate 3.9%, lowest since 2017 ex-pandemic)
job_openings._source: BLS JOLTS December 2025, Indeed Hiring Lab

labor_force_participation.trend: Declining; population control updates lowered LFPR by 0.4pp and emp-pop ratio by 0.5pp
labor_force_participation.impact_on_hiring: Slowing labor force growth coincides with slowdown in job growth, explaining stable unemployment despite weak job creation
labor_force_participation._source: BLS Employment Situation February 2026, San Francisco Fed

gdp_growth.notes: US continues to outperform other advanced economies, reflecting steady household spending, robust business investment, and fiscal support
gdp_growth._source: IMF WEO January 2026, Indeed Hiring Lab

nonfarm_payrolls.2025_revised_total: 181,000 jobs (down from initially reported 584,000; weakest since 2003 ex-recession)
nonfarm_payrolls.six_month_trend: Essentially zero net job creation over past 6 months
nonfarm_payrolls.healthcare_feb_2026: -28,000 (after averaging +30,000/month over prior 12 months, partly driven by strikes)
nonfarm_payrolls._source: BLS Employment Situation, Indeed Hiring Lab Feb 2026 Jobs Report

hiring_trends.indeed_jpi_end_2025: 4.7% above pre-pandemic (down from 11.5% at start of 2025)
hiring_trends.indeed_jpi_jan_2026: Flat; never rose above early-Dec 2025 level
hiring_trends.white_collar_recession: Tech, media, professional services well below pre-pandemic; postings 34% below Feb 2020 levels
hiring_trends.ai_jobs_exception: Job postings mentioning AI surged 130%+ above pre-pandemic; tech AI postings 45% above Feb 2020
hiring_trends.unemployed_per_opening_nov_2025: 0.91 (below 1.0 for first time since pandemic recovery)
hiring_trends.job_searches_jan_2026: Up to 31% higher than early-Dec 2025 average

wage_growth.indeed_wage_tracker_trend_2025: Posted wage growth fell from 3.4% to 2.1% over 2025
wage_growth._source: BLS Employment Cost Index Dec 2025, World at Work, Indeed Hiring Lab

key_sectors.strongest: Healthcare, Construction, Skilled Trades, AI roles
key_sectors.skilled_trades_hotbeds: Owner-operator truck driver, HVAC technician, electrical foreman among top jobs
key_sectors.white_collar_brightspots: Professional fields posted modest gains exceeding 5% in Jan 2026 vs Dec 2025

wage_growth.vs_euro_area: Much stronger than euro area (2.4%) and US (2.4%)

united_kingdom.ai_adoption: Leads peer countries with 5.6% of postings mentioning AI
united_kingdom.skills_gap: 58% of employers struggling to fill roles

key_trends.tech_talent_gap: Demand for AI engineers, cloud experts, cybersecurity far outstrips supply
key_trends.nearshoring: Western firms expanding R&D into Poland, Romania, Czechia
key_trends.pay_transparency: EU Gender Pay Transparency Directive deadline June 7, 2026
key_trends.career_changers: Two-thirds of companies plan to hire career changers (StepStone)

india.top_sectors: Insurance (+28%), BPO/ITES (+22%), Real Estate (+19%), AI-ML (+21%), Hospitality (+20%)

japan.outlook: Tight labor market expected to remain in 2026

southeast_asia.trend: Demand surging faster than talent supply
southeast_asia.skills_gap: Widening in tech, semiconductors, chemicals, digital manufacturing
southeast_asia.salary_inflation: Not expected to slow meaningfully in 2026
southeast_asia.key_shift: Skills-based hiring acceleration across Singapore, Malaysia, Philippines, Vietnam

overall.skills_gap: 45-75% of employers struggling to find qualified talent

qatar.notes: Stabilizing operations, LNG projects not yet generating significant workforce demand

global.informal_employment_2025: 51.1% (2+ billion workers, expanding faster than formal employment)
global.trade_impact: 465 million workers worldwide; 84 million workers across 71 countries face elevated risk from trade uncertainty

gdp_growth.notes: Growth rates remain below historical average. If forecasts materialize, average growth this decade will be lowest since 1960s (World Bank)
gdp_growth._source: IMF WEO October 2025, IMF WEO Update January 2026, World Bank Global Economic Prospects

employment_outlook.global_employment_growth_2025: 1.5% (53 million workers, down from previous forecast of 60 million)
employment_outlook.demographic_pressures: US high school graduates expected to peak in 2025 then decline; 1 in 6 people globally will be over 60 by 2030
employment_outlook.ai_skills_demand_growth: AI Fluency demand grown nearly 7x in two years (fastest growing skill in US postings)
employment_outlook.ai_literacy_gap: Fewer than 30% of employees feel confident working with AI tools (WEF)
employment_outlook.risks: Prolonged uncertainty, more protectionism, labor supply shocks could reduce growth
employment_outlook._source: ILO, WEF Future of Jobs 2025, IMF

overall.source: World at Work, BLS Employment Cost Index

technology.notes: Generalist roles cooling, specialized AI/cybersecurity roles seeing sharp increases
technology.economic_stability_concern: 66% of employers cited as reason for shrinking budget

healthcare.trend: Continued upward pressure due to shortages

construction_skilled_trades.labor_shortages: Pushing wages upward for licensed/certified professionals

manufacturing.smart_manufacturing_investment: 80% of executives plan 20%+ of improvement budgets
manufacturing.trend: Engineering and automation roles in high demand

finance_insurance.trend: Wage growth exceeds planned base-pay increases

gcc_middle_east.trend: Moderating but positive after post-pandemic surge (2022-2024)

global.context: New post-pandemic standard due to worldwide inflation

healthcare.hiring_strength: Strongest sector in US (75% of net job growth)

technology.recovery_signs: Tech budgets thawing for AI and efficiency tools
technology.ai_roles_exception: Growing rapidly with sharp salary increases

market_size.growth_drivers: Online job portals, competition for skilled talent, corporate hiring volumes
market_size._source: Recruitment Advertising Agency Global Market Report 2026

staffing_industry.it_staffing_outlook: Mild declines in 2025 followed by slight growth in 2026; first top-line growth since 2022 if prediction holds
staffing_industry.it_staffing_ai_disruption: AI agents supplied alongside human talent as hybrid workforce; likely to disrupt industry, increasing margins and decreasing project lengths
staffing_industry.temp_staffing_trend: BLS data shows temporary help employment grew sequentially in Nov-Jan, suggesting potential turning point
staffing_industry.growth_sectors: Healthcare, professional services, Sun Belt markets, data center construction
staffing_industry.headwinds: Sluggish overall US labor market, low voluntary turnover, policy uncertainty, geopolitical shocks, AI uncertainty
staffing_industry._source: Staffing Industry Analysts (SIA) Global Market Estimates & Forecasts 2025-2030, US Staffing Industry Forecast Sep 2025

programmatic_adoption_in_recruitment.vs_display_marketing: Widespread in display, nascent in recruitment
programmatic_adoption_in_recruitment.waste_in_job_advertising: Up to 40% of job advertising spending is wasted
programmatic_adoption_in_recruitment.programmatic_roi: ~50% reduction in cost per applicant and 33% better applicant quality
programmatic_adoption_in_recruitment.metric_evolution: Industry shifting from CPC/CPA toward CPQA (Cost Per Qualified Applicant)
programmatic_adoption_in_recruitment.ai_application_challenge: AI-generated applications flood recruiters with 10x more applications but still result in just one hire

career_sites.strengths: Brand control, lower cost, owned channel

labor_force_participation.us_trend: Declining; population control updates lowered LFPR by 0.4pp
labor_force_participation.demographic_pressure: US high school graduates expected to peak in 2025 then decline; 1 in 6 people globally will be over 60 by 2030
labor_force_participation.immigration_trend: Declining, affecting construction, hospitality, caregiving, engineering
labor_force_participation.impact: Slowing labor force growth explains how weak job creation hasn't caused sharply higher unemployment
labor_force_participation._source: BLS Employment Situation Feb 2026, San Francisco Fed, Deloitte

quality_of_hire.ai_potential: 61% believe AI can improve QoH measurement
quality_of_hire.shrm_finding: Average cost-per-hire and time-to-hire have both increased in past three years, correlating with increased use of GenAI

candidate_experience.transparency_in_ai: Defining effective hiring in 2026; candidates expect fairness and choice including ability to opt out of AI interviews (Gartner)
candidate_experience.stability_signals: Candidates paying closer attention to signals of stability, leadership, and culture (Rally 2026)
candidate_experience.ai_role_in_candidate_research: Recruitment marketing role shifts as AI tools recommend and compare employers before recruiters get involved

ai_workforce_transformation.ai_fluency_demand_growth: Nearly 7x in two years (fastest growing skill in US postings)
ai_workforce_transformation.ai_literacy_gap: Fewer than 30% of employees feel confident working with AI tools (WEF)
ai_workforce_transformation.ai_wage_premium: 56% premium for workers with AI skills in 2024 (up from 25%, PwC)
ai_workforce_transformation.occupational_transitions_by_2030: Up to 12 million each in Europe and US
ai_workforce_transformation.us_work_hours_automatable: 57% could theoretically be automated by current tech (McKinsey)
ai_workforce_transformation.ai_maturity: 88% of organizations use AI in at least one function; only 1% have achieved true maturity (McKinsey)
ai_workforce_transformation.knowledge_workers_using_ai: 75% already use AI tools, often without formal deployment (McKinsey)
ai_workforce_transformation.ai_proof_careers: 62% of workers would switch to skilled trades for better pay and stability (Gartner)
ai_workforce_transformation._source: WEF Future of Jobs 2025, PwC AI Jobs Barometer 2025, McKinsey, Gartner
